name: md-equilibrium-dynamics
description: Classical dynamics / solvation / sampling of a large or flexible system: `md build` (box + topology) then `md simulate` (EM -> NVT -> NPT -> production).
caveats: MD runs in WSL via GROMACS - confirm the GROMACS engine is configured (check `inspect`).
`md build` assigns a force field and water model automatically; make sure the topology suits your system before a long run.
Use MD for dynamics, solvation, and large flexible systems - not for bond breaking or electronic properties (use QM for those).
body: Start short to validate the setup, then extend --time. NVT equilibrates temperature, NPT equilibrates density, then production samples. For a single relaxed structure rather than a trajectory, `--no-relax` skips equilibration. Analysis (RMSD, etc.) runs after production.

name: md-vs-qm
description: Deciding QM vs MD: QM for electronic structure / energies / spectra of small systems; MD for time evolution, solvation, and conformational sampling of large ones.
caveats: Classical MD cannot break or form covalent bonds and has no electronic structure; QM cannot reach the size/timescale MD samples.
body: Rule of thumb: thousands of atoms over nanoseconds -> MD (GROMACS). Tens of atoms, energies/barriers/spectra/charges -> QM. Workflows often combine them: sample conformers or a binding pose, then refine key structures with QM.
